final_text:
Confirmed — HTAN's biospecimen schema uses entirely different (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE) field names with zero overlap. The match is unambiguous.

## Schema Identification

**Match: `BiospecimenTemplate.json`** from the `nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary` repository.

### Identifier
- **File path:** `registered-json-schemas/BiospecimenTemplate.json`
- **Schema `$id`:** `https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate`
- **Title:** `BiospecimenTemplate`

### Reasoning

Every field in the sample (`individualID`, `parentSpecimenID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, `tumorType`) appears as a property in the NF Biospecimen template. In fact, these are exactly the first five properties of the schema, and three of them (`individualID`, `specimenID`, `tumorType`) are listed as required. The schema also defines additional optional properties (`bodySite`, `experimentalCondition`, `modelSystemName`, `modelSpecies`, `modelSex`, `modelAge`, `modelAgeUnit`) that the sample omits — which is consistent with a valid partial instance.

The semantics line up with the NF dictionary's own description of these terms — e.g., parentSpecimenID is "A unique identifier (non-PII) that represents the parent specimen (sample) from which the data came from, e.g. the single parent tumor that was subsectioned into several samples. The parentSpecimenID can be the same as specimenID when there is no subsectioning."

### Why HTAN was ruled out

I checked the HTAN v1.1.0 biospecimen schema (`HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json`) for comparison. Its properties use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE naming (`HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `HTAN_PARENT_ID`, `BIOSPECIMEN_TYPE`, `ACQUISITION_METHOD_TYPE`, etc.) and have **zero** field-name overlap with the sample. None of the other HTAN v1.1.0 schemas in scope (Demographics, Diagnosis, assay-level schemas, etc.) describe specimen aliquot identifiers in this way either.

### Confidence

High and unambiguous — the field set is a clean subset of exactly one in-scope schema, and the only other plausible candidate repository uses a completely different naming convention with no overlap.

### Sources used
- File listing: `https://api.github.com/repos/nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary/contents/registered-json-schemas`
- Schema content: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary/main/registered-json-schemas/BiospecimenTemplate.json`
- HTAN comparison: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ncihtan/htan2-data-model/main/JSON_Schemas/v1.1.0/HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json`
